title: The Relevance of Attitudinal Factors for the Acceptance of Energy Policy Measures: A Micro-econometric Analysis

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: financial support of the expansion of renewable energies
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is a subsidy for renewables, not a tax instrument.

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: financial support of saving energy in households and industry
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is direct public support, not a carbon or fuel tax.
This is a regulatory phase-out, not a tax.

inclusion_reason: Empirical survey of support for German energy-transition measures, but it does not analyze any carbon, CO2, fuel, gasoline, diesel, or fossil-fuel tax.
exclusion_reason: The paper studies energy-policy measures like subsidies, phase-out, and grid expansion; taxes are only mentioned in the literature review, not analyzed as outcomes.
